steps.title: Identify what the sail hypothesis claims
steps.content: The hypothesis states the sail improved “success in underwater pursuits of prey species capable of making quick, evasive movements.” Evasive prey require the predator to make sharp, quick direction changes—maneuverability matters, not speed or efficiency alone.

steps.title: Find the result demonstrating superior maneuverability
steps.content: A sail enabling quicker, sharper turns allows the dinosaur to follow prey's abrupt movements, directly supporting the hypothesis. The other three either don't address pursuit capability or suggest diminished performance. That matches choice A — A is correct.
